stories.title: CPSC Safety Alert — Infant Walkers, Strollers, Travel Bassinets & Baby Monitors Recalled [TIER 1]

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: 4 more CPSC recalls: Uuoeebb infant walkers (fall+entrapment), AliExpress strollers (restraint fails), Tuyedoqe bassinets (Warning — no refund), Babysense monitors (fire hazard). Stop use now. cpsc.gov/Recalls 🚨 #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: The Tuyedoqe travel bassinet is a CPSC Warning, not a recall — the company refused to cooperate. No refund exists. CPSC says: stop using now, cut the cover in half, dispose of it. About 250 sold on Amazon in 2025. 🚨 #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
```
Four more children's products have been flagged by the CPSC with serious hazard warnings — and COS parents should check their homes now.

The first is the Uuoeebb Infant Walker, sold on Amazon by BaoD. The CPSC found these walkers violate the mandatory safety standard for infant walkers in three ways: they fit through a standard doorway (meaning they can reach the top of stairs before stopping), they fail to stop at step edges, and their leg openings are large enough for a child's head to slip through — creating entrapment and strangulation risk. If you bought a baby walker on Amazon in the past year, check the brand.

The second is a line of Convertible Strollers sold on AliExpress, priced between $215 and $415. The restraint system on these strollers can fail, creating a fall hazard. These were sold from September 2024 through October 2025.

Third is the situation parents most need to know about: the Tuyedoqe Travel Bassinet. The CPSC issued a Warning — not a formal recall — because the company refused to cooperate with the recall process. That means there is no official refund program and no replacement option. CPSC's guidance is direct: stop using the bassinet immediately, remove the foam padding, cut the cover in half, and dispose of both. About 250 units were sold on Amazon between April and October 2025 for around $40.

Fourth: the Babysense Max View Baby Monitor. The parent unit — the display screen — can overheat or spark when charging. This is a fire hazard, especially for parents who charge the unit near the crib overnight.

For the full recall details and remedies, visit cpsc.gov/Recalls. If you're unsure whether your product is affected, err on the side of stopping use until you can verify.

Which of these products do you have at home? Drop a comment — it helps other parents too.
```
facebook.image_caption:
#### Image Caption -- 8:30 AM MT

```
4 baby products flagged this week — infant walkers, strollers, a bassinet, and a monitor. Check cpsc.gov/Recalls.
```

article.headline: CPSC Safety Alert: 4 Baby Products Recalled or Warned — Infant Walkers, Strollers, Bassinet, Baby Monitor

stories.title: COS Easter Weekend Guide — ViewHouse Brunch, Easter Eve Food Fest, Royal Gorge Sunrise [TIER 2]

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text: Easter is April 5 in COS. ViewHouse brunch + egg hunt (9am-3pm, book now). Easter Eve Food Fest at Norris Penrose, April 4. Royal Gorge Sunrise Service Easter morning — limited seating, dress warm. 🐣 #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
```
Easter Sunday is April 5 — that's five days away. If you're planning a Colorado Springs Easter weekend for your family, here's what's happening.

ViewHouse is hosting an Easter brunch with a family-friendly egg hunt and Easter Bunny photos on Sunday, April 5 from 9 AM to 3 PM. Brunch spots fill up fast at this kind of event — if you're interested, book a reservation now rather than hoping there's walk-in availability on Sunday morning.

If you'd rather do something Saturday night, the Easter Eve Food Festival at the Norris Penrose Event Center in Old Colorado City runs April 4. It's a chocolate and cheese festival — which, if you have kids who feel strongly about either food group, is basically a custom event.

For families who want something a little more contemplative, the Royal Gorge Sunrise Service on Easter Sunday morning is one of Colorado's most distinctive traditions. The service takes place on the rim of the Royal Gorge as the sun rises over the Sangre de Cristo Mountains. Seating is limited, dress in warm layers (spring mornings at elevation are cold), and consider arriving early.

One planning note for all Easter weekend events in COS: spring weather means anything. Average highs are around 59°F but late-season snow is entirely possible. Have a backup plan for outdoor activities and keep an extra layer in the car.

What's your family's Easter weekend plan this year?
```

#### Image Caption -- 10:30 AM MT

```
Easter is April 5. ViewHouse brunch, Easter Eve Food Fest, Royal Gorge Sunrise Service — COS has options.
```

stories.title: New Study — Science Just Identified the 7 Factors That Make Play Fun for Kids [TIER 2]

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text: New study: 7 things make play fun for kids — imagination, silliness, inclusion, energy, goals, and "play feeling." Finding: forcing kids to play together makes it LESS fun. Back off, COS parents. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
```
A study published yesterday in Frontiers in Psychology asked a simple question: what actually makes it fun for kids to play together?

The researchers identified seven factors. Kids find group play most enjoyable when they feel genuinely included in the group, when there's room for imagination, when the atmosphere is a little wild and exciting, when there's something specific to do, when there's an element of silliness or mild rule-bending, when the challenge is achievable rather than impossible, and what researchers called a "play feeling" — a hard-to-define but highly recognizable sense that this is actually play and not something else.

That seventh factor, "play feeling," had the most to do with whether children described something as fun. It's the difference between a game that feels spontaneous and alive, and an activity that feels scheduled.

The finding that will probably resonate with parents the most: forcing kids to play together actually undermines the social inclusion factor. Children who feel directed or pressured to interact with a specific peer don't experience the same sense of genuine belonging — and that, according to the study, is what makes it feel less fun.

What this means practically: this Easter weekend, if you've got a group of kids and they're finding their own thing, that's probably working. The structured egg hunt is fine. But if you find yourself insisting two kids play together and it's not clicking, the research suggests stepping back is the more effective move.

Does this match what you've noticed about your own kids' play?
```
facebook.image_caption:
#### Image Caption -- 12:30 PM MT

```
New research: 7 things make play fun for kids. No. 1 finding: forcing kids to play together makes it less fun.
```

article.headline: New Research: 7 Factors That Make Play Fun for Kids — And Why Forcing It Backfires

stories.title: D49 Opening Student Success Center with New Preschool Classrooms in August 2026 [TIER 2]

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: D49 is opening a Student Success Center in August with 2 new full-day preschool classrooms for 2026-27. Registration is open now for east COS families. Visit d49.org for details. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
```
If you're looking for a preschool spot for the 2026-27 school year in Colorado Springs, School District 49 just opened something worth knowing about.

D49 is opening a new Student Success Center at the start of the 2026-27 school year — August 2026 — and with it, two brand-new full-day preschool classrooms. Those seats are now open for registration for the upcoming school year.

D49 covers the northeast and east sides of Colorado Springs — the Falcon, Peyton, Calhan, and Powers Boulevard corridor. If you're a family in that area looking for a structured, full-day preschool option, this is new capacity that wasn't available last year.

For context on the district: D49 just received a Best Practice Spotlight recognition from the Baldrige Excellence Program, which is the national quality award for organizations demonstrating outstanding management and performance practices. It's not a small award — the Baldrige program is the gold standard for institutional performance evaluation, and being selected as a Best Practice spotlight means other school districts are being directed to look at D49 as a model.

That's not just a marketing point. It's a meaningful credibility signal for families weighing school options across the city's school districts.

Visit d49.org to find out more about preschool registration and the new Student Success Center.

Are you looking at D49 schools for the 2026-27 year? What questions do you have about COS school options?
```

stories.title: April Nature Events for COS Families — Bear Creek, Birding Walk, Fountain Creek (FREE & Low-Cost) [TIER 3]

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: Free April nature event in COS: Bear Creek Early Literacy, April 17, ages birth-8, ASL-inclusive, free. Also: Fountain Creek Habitat Hike April 24, Homeschool Programs April 23 ($4). Birding walk April 9 & 11. 🌿 #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
```
April in Colorado Springs has a lot of structured outdoor programming if you know where to look — and a good chunk of it is free or costs less than a coffee.

On April 17, Bear Creek Nature Center is hosting a free Early Literacy Event designed specifically for families with deaf and hard-of-hearing children ages birth to 8. The Colorado School for the Deaf and the Blind is collaborating with Bear Creek for the program, which includes a book read in American Sign Language, wildlife activities focused on animals in summer, and a short nature walk. It runs from 1:30 to 3 PM and there's no cost to attend.

On April 23, Bear Creek runs Homeschool Nature Programs from 1 to 3:30 PM — two sessions running simultaneously for different age groups, so families with kids at multiple levels can attend together. Kids age 8 and up can be dropped off; children 7 and under need a caregiver with them. Cost is $4 per person with prepaid registration.

On April 24, Fountain Creek Regional Park is hosting a Habitat Exploration for grades 3-6 from 2 to 4 PM — a guided exploration of five habitats on the property, including a wetland activity and pond study. Also $4 per person.

If birds are your family's thing, Aiken Audubon is running an Intermediate Birding Walk on April 9 and 11 with Mary Wilson. The two-part series covers identification, behavior, habitats, and gear. It's $10 per person, $8 for members.

Spring in COS is unpredictable — check weather before heading out and dress in layers. But these programs are worth putting on the calendar now, especially the ones that require preregistration.

Which nature programs are your kids into — wildlife, plants, birding, or something else?
```
facebook.image_caption:
#### Image Caption -- 5:30 PM MT

```
Free COS nature events in April: Bear Creek Early Literacy (April 17, ages birth-8, ASL-inclusive). Plus Fountain Creek hike, homeschool programs, and birding walk.
```
